message:
feat(planner): differential view compiler + rowid-aware covering indexes + multi-term index analysis

Three closely related planner improvements that share the same
WHERE/index analysis machinery.

(1) New differential-view compiler module codifies the Bloodstream-
    compatible view shapes that future commit-time differential
    operators will consume, so the planner can fail closed on
    unsupported shapes today without having to rediscover shape
    support ad hoc later.

(2) best_access_path_internal() now treats an index as "covering"
    when the caller only needs a rowid alias, even if the rowid
    is not explicitly listed in the index column list.  Ordinary
    SQLite indexes always carry the rowid payload, so an
    index-only scan is legal when the requested output is
    {rowid, <index columns>} — the previous check missed that
    case and forced a table lookup.

(3) analyze_index_usability() is rewritten around a per-column
    term summary so the same walk computes equality-prefix length,
    first-in-probe column index, range predicates, and LIKE-prefix
    candidates in one pass instead of three.  This both fixes a
    latent bug where the previous eq-prefix scan stopped at the
    first column that had ANY term (even a non-equality term) and
    makes the later skip-scan cost model cheaper to evaluate.

crates/fsqlite-planner/src/differential.rs (new, ~1558 lines)

- DIFFERENTIAL_VIEW_PLAN_SCHEMA_VERSION: u32 = 1 — bumped on any
  breaking change to the emitted plan shape so downstream consumers
  (the future runtime operator compiler, the harness auditor) can
  refuse to load plans at an older schema.
- DifferentialPlanMode { RowSet, GroupedAggregate, GlobalAggregate }
  classifies a SELECT into one of the three shapes the runtime
  will know how to compile.  Everything outside these three shapes
  (window functions, recursive CTEs, UNION, LIMIT-with-offset on
  aggregates, etc.) is rejected.
- Per-shape plan structs carry the projection list, grouping keys,
  aggregate functions (normalized to the internal
  DifferentialAggFn enum: Count, Sum, Min, Max, Avg, Total), join
  topology (inner/left/cross), WHERE predicates (only constants,
  column references, equality, AND/OR), and ORDER BY / LIMIT
  where applicable.
- Fail-closed compile_select(stmt) -> Result<DifferentialPlan,
  DifferentialCompileError> that walks the AST and either produces
  a plan or returns a structured error naming the exact unsupported
  construct, so the planner can log the reason when falling back
  to non-differential execution.
- Extensive unit tests covering: empty SELECT, SELECT *, WHERE
  equality, WHERE range (rejected for RowSet, accepted for
  GroupedAggregate fallback), INNER JOIN, LEFT JOIN, cross join,
  GROUP BY single column, GROUP BY composite, HAVING (rejected),
  DISTINCT (rejected), LIMIT (shape-dependent), COUNT(*),
  SUM/MIN/MAX/AVG, nested CTEs (rejected), window functions
  (rejected), recursive WITH (rejected).

crates/fsqlite-planner/src/lib.rs

- pub mod differential; registered alphabetically between
  decision_contract and stats.

- best_access_path_internal() covering-index check:
    Before: needed.iter().all(|c| idx.columns.any(|ic| eq_ic(ic, c)))
    After:  for each needed column, require either an index-column
            match *or* is_rowid_alias_name(column).  Inline comment
            explains that ordinary SQLite indexes carry the rowid
            payload so "index-only scan for rowid" is legal.

- New IndexColumnTermSummary struct { has_equality,
  first_in_probe_count: Option<usize>, has_range, first_like_prefix:
  Option<(String, Option<String>)> } — one entry per index column.

- is_rowid_column(WhereColumn) now delegates to the new
  is_rowid_alias_name(&str) helper so the "rowid / _rowid_ / oid"
  triple is defined once and shared between the WHERE analyzer and
  the covering-index check.

- analyze_index_usability() rewritten:
    - Allocate column_summaries: Vec<IndexColumnTermSummary> sized
      to index.columns.len().
    - Single pass over `terms` that, for each term with a matching
      column, updates the corresponding summary's has_equality /
      has_range / first_like_prefix / first_in_probe_count fields
      and captures leftmost_first_constraint for stable tie-breaking.
    - Derive eq_columns by scanning column_summaries left-to-right
      until has_equality becomes false, which is correct even when
      interleaved non-equality terms exist on the same column.
    - The later range / LIKE-prefix / skip-scan / first-in-probe
      logic all reads from column_summaries instead of re-walking
      `terms`, so the complexity drops from O(index_cols × terms × kinds)
      to O(index_cols + terms).

- New tests in the existing mod tests:
    - covering index accepts rowid alias even when the rowid is
      absent from idx.columns.
    - analyze_index_usability returns the correct eq_columns count
      for an index where only a middle column has a range term
      (pre-fix: terminated early; post-fix: stops at that column).
    - is_rowid_alias_name recognizes "rowid", "_rowid_", "oid" in
      any casing but rejects substrings ("rowid_alias").

No user-visible planner behavior change outside of the two focused
fixes above; the differential-view module is wired into the public
module tree but no runtime code calls compile_select() yet (the
commit-time operator compiler lands in a follow-up bead).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
